title: ophiorrhiza coriaceifolia
intro: Ophiorrhiza coriaceifolia, also known as the 'Malabar teacake', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ophiorrhiza coriaceifolia include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, and is also used in traditional Indian medicine to treat various ailments.
